---
title: "EPA CDX Upload and Data Management Procedures"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# EPA CDX Upload and Data Management Procedures {#cdx-upload}

This chapter documents the step-by-step procedures for submitting Kenai River baseline water quality data to the EPA Central Data Exchange (CDX) / Water Quality Exchange (WQX), and for removing or correcting previously submitted data.

**CDX login URL:** <https://cdx.epa.gov/>

**KWF Organization ID in EPA WQX:** `Kenai_WQX`

The three output files ready for upload are generated by `appendix_a.qmd` and written to `other/output/wqx_formatted/`:

| File | Contents |
|---------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| `results_activities.csv` | All sample results and associated activity metadata |
| `project.csv` | Project description and QAPP approval status |
| `station.csv` | Monitoring location metadata |

Upload these files together in the same CDX submission session. Also have the QAPP PDF on hand for attachment (see @sec-upload-project below):

`KenaiWatershedForum_QAPP_v3_2023_with_Addendum_April_2024.pdf`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Uploading Data to EPA CDX {#sec-upload}

### Step 1: Log in to CDX

1.  Navigate to <https://cdx.epa.gov/>.
2.  Log in with your CDX username and password. Create an account unique to your name if you do not have one yet.
3.  From the **My CDX** dashboard, locate **WQX Web** under "My Programs" and click **Go to WQX Web**.

### Step 2: Upload the Project file {#sec-upload-project}

The project file establishes the monitoring project record under which all results will be organized.

1.  In WQX Web, click **Submit** → **Import File**.
2.  Select import type: **Project**.
3.  Select the import configuration associated with KWF's project template (or create one if uploading for the first time — see EPA's WQX Web user guide for configuration setup instructions).
4.  Browse to and select `other/output/wqx_formatted/project.csv`.
5.  After the file parses successfully, attach the QAPP PDF:
    -   In the submission page, locate the **Attachments** section.
    -   Upload `KenaiWatershedForum_QAPP_v3_2023_with_Addendum_April_2024.pdf`.
    -   Set attachment type to **QAPP**.
6.  Click **Submit**. Monitor the submission status until it shows **Completed** (not just *Accepted*).

### Step 3: Upload the Station file

The station file registers monitoring location metadata (coordinates, location descriptions, etc.).

1.  In WQX Web, click **Submit** → **Import File**.
2.  Select import type: **Monitoring Location**.
3.  Select the KWF station import configuration.
4.  Browse to and select `other/output/wqx_formatted/station.csv`.
5.  Click **Submit** and wait for **Completed** status.

### Step 4: Upload the Results and Activities file

This is the main data file containing all sample results.

1.  In WQX Web, click **Submit** → **Import File**.
2.  Select import type: **Activity** (results are nested under activities in WQX).
3.  Select the KWF results/activities import configuration.
4.  Browse to and select `other/output/wqx_formatted/results_activities.csv`.
5.  Click **Submit** and wait for **Completed** status. This file is the largest and may take several minutes to process.

### Step 5: Verify the upload

After all three files have completed:

1.  In WQX Web, navigate to **Search** → **Activities** and filter by Organization ID `Kenai_WQX` and the relevant date range to confirm records are visible.
2.  As an end-to-end check, allow 24–48 hours for data to propagate, then search for KWF data in [How's My Waterway](https://mywaterway.epa.gov/) or the [Water Quality Portal](https://www.waterqualitydata.us/). Confirm that 2021 results are visible and that values, units, and site names look correct.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Removing or Correcting Previously Submitted Data {#sec-delete}

Data may need to be removed from CDX when errors are discovered in previously uploaded results (e.g., incorrect units, wrong site assignments, results that should have been flagged as Rejected). This section documents how to delete existing submissions.

> **Important:** Deletion in WQX is permanent. Always confirm the scope of what needs to be deleted before proceeding. Keep a local copy of the original submission files.

### Option A: Delete individual Activities (and their Results) in WQX Web

Activities are the parent records in WQX; deleting an activity also deletes all results nested under it.

1.  Log in to CDX and navigate to WQX Web.
2.  Click **Search** → **Activities**.
3.  Filter by:
    -   **Organization ID:** `Kenai_WQX`
    -   **Date range:** the sample collection dates you want to remove
    -   Optionally filter by **Monitoring Location ID** or **Characteristic Name** to narrow scope
4.  Review the list of matching activities to confirm you are targeting the correct records.
5.  Select the activities to delete (checkbox) and click **Delete Selected**.
6.  Confirm the deletion when prompted. Wait for the operation to complete.

### Option B: Delete an entire annual dataset by submitting a delete transaction

If you need to remove all records for a given year (e.g., to re-upload a corrected 2021 dataset), it is more efficient to delete by Activity ID prefix.

1.  In WQX Web, use **Search** → **Activities**, filter to the target year, and select all.
2.  Click **Delete Selected** and confirm.

Alternatively, WQX supports deletion via an XML transaction file — contact EPA Region 10 WQX support if you need to delete large volumes of records programmatically.

### Correcting data without full deletion

For minor corrections (e.g., changing a `Result Status ID` from Accepted to Rejected for specific records), you do not need to delete and re-upload. WQX will overwrite existing records when you re-upload a file containing the same Activity ID. Ensure the Activity IDs in your corrected file exactly match those already in WQX.

### Removing the existing 2021 KWF submission

As of March 2026, the 2021 KWF dataset has been previously uploaded to CDX but contains errors (unit errors for Ca/Mg/Fe, site ID mismatch for RM 1.5). Corrected output files have been regenerated (see @appendix_a for details). To replace the 2021 data:

1.  Follow **Option A** or **B** above to delete all existing 2021 activities for organization `Kenai_WQX`.
2.  Verify deletion is complete by searching for activities in the 2021 date range and confirming zero results are returned.
3.  Re-upload all three corrected files following @sec-upload above.
4.  Verify the corrected data in How's My Waterway.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Troubleshooting Common CDX Issues

| Issue | Likely Cause | Resolution |
|------------------|----------------------------|--------------------------|
| Import fails with "invalid monitoring location" | Station not yet registered in WQX | Upload `station.csv` before `results_activities.csv` |
| Import fails with "method speciation required" | A characteristic requires a speciation value (e.g., phosphorus via ICP/MS) | Add the appropriate speciation value to the import file or flag the records as Rejected |
| Records submitted but not visible in WQP | Propagation delay, or status is "Accepted" not "Completed" | Wait 24–48 hours; recheck submission status in WQX Web |
| Duplicate Activity IDs | Re-uploading without deleting first | WQX will overwrite on duplicate Activity ID; verify the overwrite was applied correctly |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## EPA and WQX Resources

-   **CDX login:** <https://cdx.epa.gov/>
-   **WQX Web user guide:** <https://www.epa.gov/waterdata/water-quality-exchange-web-wqx-web-user-guide>
-   **WQX domain values (valid characteristic names, units, etc.):** <https://www.epa.gov/waterdata/wqx-domain-files>
-   **Water Quality Portal:** <https://www.waterqualitydata.us/>
-   **How's My Waterway:** <https://mywaterway.epa.gov/>
-   **EPA Region 10 WQX support contact:** Contact EPA Region 10 Water Quality Exchange coordinator for organization-specific questions.
